verdict: A — Same failure class, B — Different neighbour-column patterns
failureClass: Pass C numeric column mis-identification on dense multi-column restaurant invoice tables

prosciutto.mechanism: Alternates between reading Preço Unit (10,30→10.17), net unit (8.17), Desc.(%) (17→17.50), and derived values (8.2)
prosciutto.columnShiftEvidence: Run 3 unit_price=17 matches Desc.(%) 17,50 on source row
prosciutto.bestRun: Run 4: unit_price 8.17 (matches VL GT net), total 36.54 (matches visible Preço Total)

pomodor.mechanism: Alternates between reading DESC 20,00% as €20 (run 1) and P.VENDA 27,560 as unit_price (runs 2,4,5); totals inconsistent (40, 54.2, 42.2, 20.02)
pomodor.columnShiftEvidence: Run 1 unit_price=20 exact match to DESC column; runs 2/4/5 unit_price=27.56 matches P.VENDA
pomodor.stableRefinedValidation: Prior passc-refinement-validation run showed stable 20/40; this audit's 5 runs show 3 unit prices and 4 totals

sharedVsDifferent.shared: Both fail at Pass C only; both involve discount/% column adjacent to unit-price column; neither caused by geometry/footer/persistence
sharedVsDifferent.different: Prosciutto shifts among gross price, net price, and discount %; Pomodor shifts between discount % and list P.VENDA; qty semantics differ (4.3 kg vs pack *6)
